question.text: For redemption of equity/debt scheme units, if the application is received before 3 PM, the applicable NAV is:

answer.text: The closing NAV of the previous business day
The average of the previous and current day's NAV
The closing NAV of the same business day
The closing NAV of the next business day

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For redemption requests received before the 3 PM cut-off in equity/debt schemes, the applicable NAV is the closing NAV of the same business day. If received after 3 PM, the next business day's closing NAV applies. **The Trap:** Redemption NAV rules are simpler than purchase rules -- there is no 'funds available' condition since the investor is selling units, not buying. The cut-off time is the only determining factor. **Cross-Exam Context:** The asymmetry between purchase NAV (needs funds availability) and redemption NAV (only needs application time) is a key exam point. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client wants to redeem at a specific NAV (e.g., on a day when markets are up), ensure the redemption request is submitted well before 3 PM. After 3 PM, they get the next day's NAV which could be lower if markets fall.
